Details: Removal of perchlorate by membrane filtration is not effective in natural waters. Studies with ultrafiltration membrane coated with polyamide showed less than 3 to 80 percent of perchlorate removal. The high percent removals were in distilled-deionized water due to membrane charge rejection. This mechanism disappeared in natural waters resulting in poor removals in all such cases. Membrane filtration is not effective for perchlorate rejection mostly because the membrane's pore size is orders of magnitude larger than the perchlorate molecule.
